# The data

**All of it is synthetic.** Every transcript in these files was generated by
`make_data.py` from sentence templates. No person said any of these words. There is no
IRB protocol, no consent form, and no PHI here ŌĆö which is exactly why we can spend three
hours pasting it into chatbots.

It is designed to *behave* like a real picture-description dataset: severity degrades
lexical content, the group difference is real but not absurd, and there is one measure
(`type_token_ratio`) that looks useful and mostly isn't. That last one is on purpose.

## transcripts.csv ŌĆö 60 rows, one per participant

| Column | Type | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `participant_id` | chr | `C###` control, `A###` aphasia |
| `group` | chr | `control` or `aphasia` |
| `age` | int | years |
| `sex` | chr | `F` / `M` |
| `education_years` | int | years of formal education |
| `months_post_onset` | dbl | months since stroke; `NA` for controls |
| `wab_aq` | dbl | Aphasia Quotient, 0ŌĆō100. Higher = less impaired. Controls sit at ~99 (ceiling) |
| `transcript` | chr | their spoken description of the picture |

## transcripts_long.csv ŌĆö 60 rows, 30 participants ├Ś 2 visits

The aphasia group only, measured `acute` (~1 month post-onset) and `chronic`
(~12 months). Same columns plus `timepoint`. **These are repeated measures** ŌĆö 60 rows,
30 people. Any model that ignores that is wrong.

## features.csv ŌĆö 60 rows, the answers to Part 1

The NLP measures pre-computed, so the R half works even if you never finished the Python
half. Columns: `n_words`, `n_unique_words`, `type_token_ratio`, `mean_word_length`,
`content_word_ratio`, `filler_rate`.

## What's actually in the signal

Within the aphasia group, correlations with WAB-AQ:

| Measure | r |
|---|---|
| `filler_rate` | **ŌłÆ0.83** |
| `n_words` | +0.60 |
| `content_word_ratio` | +0.51 |
| `mean_word_length` | +0.46 |
| `type_token_ratio` | +0.17 ŌåÉ the trap |

Type-token ratio falls as transcripts get longer, and impaired speakers produce shorter
transcripts. So TTR is fighting itself. It is a real, well-documented confound, and no AI
assistant will mention it unless you ask.

## recovery_prediction.csv ŌĆö 90 synthetic acute-stroke participants

This file supports the advanced Python notebook. Each row is one participant assessed
in the acute phase. The outcome is `outcome_wab_aq_12m`, a synthetic 12-month WAB-AQ.
The other variables are available at the acute assessment:

| Column | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `acute_wab_aq` | acute WAB-AQ |
| `acute_discourse_score` | synthetic acute narrative-discourse score (0ŌĆō10) |
| `lesion_volume_ml` | synthetic lesion volume in millilitres |
| `cortical_lesion_pct` | synthetic cortical lesion burden (%) |
| `dorsal_disconnection_pct` | synthetic dorsal-stream disconnection proportion |
| `ventral_disconnection_pct` | synthetic ventral-stream disconnection proportion |

The file is an exercise in prediction workflow: define predictors available at baseline,
keep the 12-month outcome out of the predictor matrix, and evaluate models with resampling.
It is not a clinical prediction model, a representation of a trial cohort, or evidence for
any clinical association.
